name: lion's mane
mechanism: Mechanism is not yet established with high confidence; reported activity is likely multi-component and may vary by extract chemistry and dose.
interactions: May potentiate: May potentiate stimulant effects when combined with sympathomimetic agents
safetyNotes: Safety profile depends on species identity, preparation quality, dose, and co-medications; start low, avoid prolonged high-dose use without supervision, and stop if adverse effects occur.

sources.title: PubMed search: lion's mane
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Lion’s mane is commonly chosen for cognitive-support routines where users prefer mushroom-based daily formats over stimulant-heavy products.
dosage: Compare fruiting-body disclosure and per-serving extract amounts rather than relying on front-label mushroom claims.
duration: Typically evaluated across multi-week routines to judge consistency and tolerability.
therapeuticUses: Mushroom extract comparisons centered on label transparency
categoryUseContext: Compared for focus and mental-clarity support in daytime routines, usually as capsules or powdered extracts.
evidenceLevel: Early-to-moderate human evidence with stronger preclinical interest; product sourcing and extract quality vary widely.
